description: Rules governing hidden truth: secret roles, bluffing, logical inference, and the flow of concealed information.

hidden_roles.description: Secret identities and allegiances assigned to players.

focuses.secret_identities: Players are secretly dealt roles or team allegiances that shape their goals while others must figure them out (The Resistance, Werewolf).
focuses.traitor_mechanics: A hidden traitor works against the group from within, sabotaging while avoiding exposure (Shadows over Camelot, Dead of Winter's betrayer).
focuses.role_powers_night_actions: Roles confer secret abilities used during hidden phases such as night (the Seer in Werewolf, night abilities in Blood on the Clocktower).
focuses.role_reveal_timing: Rules govern when identities become public: on death, on accusation, at game end, or never (role cards flipped on execution in Werewolf variants).
focuses.hidden_role_distribution: Not every role is dealt into play, so which ones are actually present is itself uncertain (the three center cards in One Night Ultimate Werewolf).
focuses.mid_game_allegiance_change: Players can switch teams during play through conversion, recruitment, or sleeper triggers (the sleeper-agent phase in Battlestar Galactica).
focuses.self_role_uncertainty: A player may not reliably know their own role or may lose track of it (the Drunk in Blood on the Clocktower, swapped masks in Mascarade).
focuses.role_swapping: Actions exchange roles between players or with hidden pools, scrambling who is what (the Troublemaker in One Night Ultimate Werewolf).
focuses.independent_agenda_roles: Some roles belong to no team and pursue private win conditions, distorting both sides' reads (the Tanner in Werewolf variants).
focuses.dead_player_participation: Eliminated players retain limited participation such as a final vote or whispered information rather than leaving the game (ghost votes in Blood on the Clocktower).

bluffing.description: Making, selling, and challenging claims that may be false.

focuses.bluff_claims: Players openly claim capabilities or holdings that may be false, gaining the benefit unless contested (role claims in Coup).
focuses.challenge_calls: A stated claim can be formally challenged, with heavy stakes falling on whichever side is wrong (challenges in Coup).
focuses.misdirection_actions: Players take deliberately suboptimal or misleading actions to disguise their role, position, or intent (a hidden traitor conspicuously helping the team to preserve cover).
focuses.goods_declaration_inspection: Players declare hidden goods and an authority may pay to inspect, with penalties for whichever party lied or guessed wrong (Sheriff of Nottingham).
focuses.hidden_pool_bid_bluffing: Players bid on the combined contents of everyone's concealed pools, mixing probability with bravado (Liar's Dice).
focuses.face_down_commitment_bluffs: Players commit pieces or cards face down, staking value on whether others believe the implied claim (the roses and skulls of Skull).
focuses.decoys_and_ambushes: Hidden pieces may be traps or worthless decoys, punishing opponents who engage the wrong target (Stratego's bombs, face-down ambushes in Android: Netrunner).
focuses.sanctioned_lying_scope: Rules define exactly what players may lie about versus what must be answered truthfully (Blood on the Clocktower permits lying about nearly everything, while other games make stated deals binding).

inference.description: Reasoning from clues and evidence toward hidden truths.

focuses.elimination_grids: Players cross off possibilities from a finite matrix until only the truth remains (the suspect, weapon, and room grid of Clue).
focuses.question_asking: Structured queries extract yes/no or categorical information from other players or the system (Guess Who?).
focuses.coordinate_deduction: Hidden positions on a grid are located by probing coordinates and interpreting hits and misses (Battleship, Captain Sonar).
focuses.code_breaking: A hidden code or rule is deduced through repeated guesses scored with structured feedback (Mastermind, Turing Machine).
focuses.distributed_clue_triangulation: Each player holds one piece of the puzzle, and the solution emerges by triangulating across everyone's constraints (Cryptid).
focuses.experiment_hypothesis_testing: Players spend actions running tests whose results confirm or eliminate hypotheses (mixing ingredients in Alchemists).
focuses.action_trail_inference: Hidden information leaks through the visible actions a player is forced to take (deducing Mr. X's position from the transport tickets he spends in Scotland Yard).
focuses.associative_clue_interpretation: Clues are indirect associations that must be interpreted rather than logically decoded (one-word clues in Codenames, vision cards in Mysterium).
focuses.negative_inference: What was not said, clued, or done carries information as strong as what was (reading which of your cards were pointedly not clued in Hanabi).
focuses.alibi_timeline_reconstruction: Players reconstruct a sequence of past events from fragmentary testimony and evidence (case timelines in Detective: A Modern Crime Board Game, mystery TTRPG scenarios).

information.description: How hidden information is structured, revealed, and paid for.

focuses.asymmetric_knowledge: Different players see systematically different slices of the game state, and play revolves around exploiting or bridging that gap (the GM-player split in TTRPGs).
focuses.one_vs_many_hidden: A single concealed player is hunted by a team that must pool observations to corner them (Scotland Yard, Fury of Dracula).
focuses.clue_revelation_pacing: Information is released on a designed schedule that controls the tension curve (one vision card per round in Mysterium, timed hint systems in escape-room games).
focuses.triggered_forced_reveals: Specific actions or states compel a hidden player to disclose information (crossing a searcher's line of sight in Specter Ops).
focuses.sealed_solution_setup: The truth is fixed secretly at setup, often sealed away unseen by anyone, and the game is the race to reconstruct it (the solution envelope in Clue).
focuses.paid_information_actions: Gaining information costs actions, resources, or position, making knowledge an economy (scan actions in The Search for Planet X).
focuses.fog_of_war_units: Units' identities or strengths stay hidden from opponents until contact forces a reveal (Stratego, block wargames like Hammer of the Scots).
focuses.verifiable_secret_logs: A hidden player keeps a written record of secret moves that can be audited later for honesty (Mr. X's travel log in Scotland Yard).
focuses.moderator_or_app_referee: A neutral moderator or companion app tracks hidden state and adjudicates secretly (the Werewolf moderator, the Alchemists app).
focuses.accusation_endgame: The game climaxes in a formal accusation or final guess, with wrong answers costly or eliminating (final accusations in Clue).
